channel.title: Tau. Claim, Doubt, Verdict
channel.description: A conversation in three voices about decidable, self-referential governance: one claims, one doubts — and the third voice is a machine that only ever speaks computed verdicts. Every claim is runnable: github.com/taumorrow/tau-lang-demos — Voices: Piper TTS (RyanSpeech CC BY-NC-SA, Lessac Blizzard 2013, Alan/Mycroft).
channel.itunes:author: Taumorrow
channel.itunes:summary: A conversation in three voices about decidable, self-referential governance: one claims, one doubts — and the third voice is a machine that only ever speaks computed verdicts. Every claim is runnable: github.com/taumorrow/tau-lang-demos — Voices: Piper TTS (RyanSpeech CC BY-NC-SA, Lessac Blizzard 2013, Alan/Mycroft).

item.title: The impossible, honestly examined
item.itunes:title: The impossible, honestly examined
item.description: Provably safe rules: usually a marketing promise, here a computation. What the claim is, what is verified, and where the asterisks live.

item.title: Episode 1: The one question nobody can decide
item.itunes:title: Episode 1: The one question nobody can decide
item.description: Does a system keep its rule through every self-change? Why that question is provably undecidable for normal programs, and why every tool gives up one ingredient.

item.title: Episode 2: The trade
item.itunes:title: Episode 2: The trade
item.description: Tau hands back the Turing superpowers and gets decidability. The barber, Goedel, atomless algebra and the guarded successor, in plain language.

item.title: Episode 3: The living constitution
item.itunes:title: Episode 3: The living constitution
item.description: A body of rules whose only job is to govern its own change: the core sticks, the body breathes. Why a thousand voices are algebraically one, and where Suber waits.

item.title: Episode 4: When you actually need Tau
item.itunes:title: Episode 4: When you actually need Tau
item.description: The most honest episode: when does SAT suffice, and when is Tau truly necessary? Two reductions, three ingredients, and the scarce one is path dependence.

item.title: Episode 5: The ratchet
item.itunes:title: Episode 5: The ratchet
item.description: Seal or replace, the order decides: same ingredients, two verdicts. Path dependence at the binary, and why no snapshot can see it.

item.title: Episode 6: The worked example
item.itunes:title: Episode 6: The worked example
item.description: The attacker takes over every vote and still loses: a sealed cap as an empty intersection instead of a promise.

item.title: Episode 7: A constitution you can touch
item.itunes:title: Episode 7: A constitution you can touch
item.description: Nine people, one board, one basic right: a small nonprofit governs its own charter. A hierarchy of norms with a telescope effect, separation of powers as a one-liner, four hard limits.

item.title: Episode 8: Decision without voting
item.itunes:title: Episode 8: Decision without voting
item.description: Deciding without counting heads: conditions instead of votes, a ranking instead of deadlock, and in the end the smallest possible vote.

item.title: Episode 9: Securing AI agents, and where Tau does not help
item.itunes:title: Episode 9: Securing AI agents, and where Tau does not help
item.description: Rules an agent cannot argue away: five out of eight versus zero out of eight. And the most honest passage: where Tau contributes almost nothing.

item.title: Episode 10: What Tau does not solve
item.itunes:title: Episode 10: What Tau does not solve
item.description: What stays forever: formalization and the outermost anchor. What is work: maturity, scale, external review. And the anomaly, resolved.

item.title: Episode 11: The engineering truths
item.itunes:title: Episode 11: The engineering truths
item.description: Three confessions, one fork in the road, and the meta mistake: build rules for everyone who tries this themselves. Do not guess, measure.

item.title: Episode 12: Is it a revolution?
item.itunes:title: Episode 12: Is it a revolution?
item.description: Capability proven, revolution not: the net balance, the five-point list, and why a skeleton is more than nothing.

item.title: Bonus: Who amends the amendment rule?
item.itunes:title: Bonus: Who amends the amendment rule?
item.description: The bonus episode: the Basic Law skeleton, a coup in two moves, self-binding, and the proven trilemma behind it all.
